name: Steel Browser
tagline: Open-source browser API that gives AI agents a managed Chrome to automate the web
seoTitle: Steel Browser - Open-Source Browser API for AI Agents
metaDescription: Steel Browser is an open-source browser API and sandbox for AI agents. Manage sessions, proxies, and stealth, and drive Chrome with Puppeteer, Playwright, or Selenium.
overview: Steel Browser is an open-source browser API that lets AI apps and agents interact with the web without building automation infrastructure from scratch. Under the hood it runs Chrome through Puppeteer and the Chrome DevTools Protocol, and handles the parts that are usually tedious: browser processes, sessions, pages, and cleanup.

It is aimed at developers building web agents and browser-automation tools who want to focus on their AI logic rather than on keeping headless browsers alive. You can connect to it with Puppeteer, Playwright, or Selenium, or call its REST API directly from any language.

As a computer-and-browser-use tool, Steel sits between your agent and a live Chrome instance. It keeps browser state such as cookies and local storage across requests, manages proxies for IP rotation, and exposes helpers to turn pages into markdown, screenshots, or PDFs.
features: Full Chrome control via Puppeteer and CDP, usable from Puppeteer, Playwright, or Selenium
Session management that keeps browser state, cookies, and local storage across requests
Built-in proxy chain management for IP rotation
Anti-detection through stealth plugins and fingerprint management
Quick Actions endpoints to convert pages to markdown, readability, screenshots, or PDFs
Built-in request logging plus a UI to view and debug sessions

gettingStarted.intro: The quickest way to run Steel locally is the pre-built Docker image, then create a session against the local API.

steps.heading: Run the Docker image
steps.body: Pull and run the combined API + UI image. The server starts on port 3000 (UI at /ui) and port 9223 is used for the console debugger.

steps.heading: Create a session
steps.body: Use the SDK to create a browser session against your local instance. Each session keeps its own state and returns an id you can connect to.

steps.heading: Or use a Quick Action
steps.body: If you just need page content, call a Quick Actions endpoint directly without managing a session yourself.

useCases: Give an AI agent a managed browser to navigate sites, fill forms, and click through flows
Scrape or convert pages to markdown, screenshots, or PDFs for downstream LLM processing
Run authenticated automation that persists cookies and local storage across requests
Rotate IPs with built-in proxy support to reach sites that block datacenter traffic
